Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

- World model: Bar moves opposite to player, can't be destroyed permanently. White block and bar always on opposite sides. Targets convert when adjacent but revert when player moves away. Level not complete after 110 actions. Goal hypothesis: Maybe need to visit all targets in sequence and press SPACE at each. Or maybe need to reach a specific "goal" position.
- Goal model: Navigate the player to interact with the targets. Likely need to reach the center target first.
- Plan: Try moving to left target and pressing SPACE there to see if something different happens.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.

Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 122 actions; a strong score needs about 71 or fewer. You are 1.7x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.content:
Looking at the current state:
- Player: green at cols 21-23, white at col 20 (rows 32-35)
- Left target: dark gray (G) at cols 16-19 (converted because player is adjacent)
- Center target: charcoal (c) at cols 28-31 (not adjacent)
- Top-right target: charcoal (c) at cols 44-47 (not adjacent)
- Bar: at cols 32-39 (moved right as player moved left)
The left target is converted (G) because the player is adjacent. The other targets are charcoal.
I notice that when I pressed SPACE x2 at the left target, the left target stayed dark gray (G). This is different from before when the center target would revert when the player moved away.
Wait, actually the player is still adjacent to the left target. The player is at cols 20-23 and the left target is at cols 16-19. They're adjacent (gap of 0 columns). So the left target is dark gray because the player is adjacent.
Let me try moving the player away from the left target and see if it reverts. If it doesn't revert, then the SPACE action "locked" the converted state.

So I don't know if the SPACE action locked the state or if the target is just dark gray because the player is adjacent.
Let me move the player away from the left target and see if it reverts.

The left target is G (dark gray) and the other targets are c (charcoal). The player is at cols 20-23, which is adjacent to the left target at cols 16-19. So the left target is dark gray because the player is adjacent.
But wait, earlier when I was at the center target and pressed SPACE x2, the center target was dark gray. Then when I moved away, it reverted to charcoal. So the SPACE action doesn't lock the state.
Let me try a different approach. What if the goal is to have ALL THREE targets converted simultaneously? But that's impossible because the targets are far apart.
Unless... there's a way to convert all three targets at once. Maybe by pressing SPACE at a specific position where all three targets are "in range"?
Or maybe the goal is to convert the targets in a specific order, and the level completes after the third conversion. But I've converted the center target multiple times and the level doesn't complete.
Wait, let me re-read the output. After SPACE x2 at the left target:
- Left target: G (dark gray)
- Center target: c (charcoal)
- Top-right target: c (charcoal)
The left target is dark gray because the player is adjacent. The other targets are charcoal because the player is not adjacent.
But earlier, when I was at the center target and pressed SPACE x2, the center target was dark gray. Then when I moved away, it reverted to charcoal.
So the pattern is:
- Targets convert to dark gray when the player is adjacent
- Targets revert to charcoal when the player moves away
- SPACE doesn't lock the state
This means I can't have all three targets converted simultaneously.
OK, let me try a completely different hypothesis. What if the goal is NOT about the targets at all? What if the goal is about the bar?
The bar:
- Moves in the opposite direction of the player
- Shrinks when the white block covers it
- Regrows when the player moves away
What if the goal is to shrink the bar to a specific size and then stop? Or what if the goal is to move the bar to a specific position?
Actually, I just realized something. The bar is at rows 28-31, which is the SAME rows as the player (when the player is at rows 28-31). The bar moves in the opposite direction of the player. So the bar and player are always on the same rows, but on opposite sides of the center.

Actually, I'm at cols 20-23 now. Let me move further left to see if the bar moves further right.

Or what if the pink bar is a "progress" bar that fills up as I complete objectives? Let me check if it's shrinking or growing.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move to top-right target: rows 24-27, cols 44-47
# Current: player at rows 32-35, cols 20-23
# Need to move UP 1 (to rows 28-31), then UP 1 more (to rows 24-27)
# Then RIGHT to reach cols 44-47

# First move UP to align rows
result = action(['UP', 'UP'])
print(f"After UP x2: reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  Player: color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Now move RIGHT to reach top-right target at cols 44-47
# Player at cols 20-23, need to reach cols 44-47
# Move RIGHT 24 times to get to cols 44-47

result = action(['RIGHT']*20)
print(f"\nAfter RIGHT x20: reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  Player: color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check top-right target
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'G'] and n['pixels'] == 12:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        if min(rows) <= 27:
            print(f"  Top-right target: color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Move RIGHT more to reach target
result = action(['RIGHT']*5)
print(f"\nAfter RIGHT x5 more: reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  Player: color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check top-right target
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'G'] and n['pixels'] == 12:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        if min(rows) <= 27:
            print(f"  Top-right target: color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Press SPACE at top-right target
result = action(['SPACE', 'SPACE'])
print(f"\nAfter SPACE x2 at top-right target: reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'G'] and n['pixels'] == 12:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  Target: color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

        rows = [p[0] for p in b]

        print(f"  Player: color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check top-right target
for n in seg['nodes']:

        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        if min(rows) <= 27:
            print(f"  Top-right target: color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Press SPACE at top-right target
result = action(['SPACE', 'SPACE'])
print(f"\nAfter SPACE x2 at top-right target: reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
